text:
Start × Spell × Town of Prizes (スタート×呪文×懸賞の街, Sutāto × Jumon × Kenshō no Machi) is the 74 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 4 th episode of the Greed Island OVA series. It was released on DVD on March 5 th , 2003.

Overview

During the game's introduction, Gon finally finds out the contents of the memory card Ging left him. Together, Killua and Gon take their first steps into Greed Island. They go to the town of Antokiba and get their first cards. Later, they see a man who entered Greed Island with them die from a mysterious explosion.

Synopsis

Gon arrives at the end of the hall, where he is greeted by Eta, who scans his face and can identify him. Eta then plays a message for Gon left for him by his father Ging, in which Ging tells Gon to have fun while he is in the game, as there are no clues in Greed Island that will lead his son to him, and that he only lead Gon there to show off his game. Gon is immediately surprised by this, but doesn't completely trust his father's words.

Eta then outlines the basics of playing Greed Island to Gon. Gon does his best to remember everything Eta tells him, but struggles with the overwhelming amount of immediate information. When Eta is finished, she opens up a staircase leading down into the world of Greed Island, and Gon's game commences. As Gon exits the Shiso Tree, he is amazed by the vast and bright world around him. However, he quickly hears voices coming from somewhere and becomes aware that he is being watched, although he does not know from where.

Other players exit the tree as well, who also notice that they're being watched. Eventually, Killua comes out to reunite with Gon, and Gon tells him how, despite there being nothing around, everyone else who exited the tree went off in the same two directions. Killua notices that the two directions are where the sense of being watched is strongest and makes an educated guess that the surveillance occurs in a city or some other similar area. To decide which of the two directions they will go in, the boys play rock paper scissors, which Killua loses. Gon chooses their direction, and they head off. Killua asks Gon if his saved data allowed him to start with cards already in his book or some helpful hints, and he is shocked and annoyed to hear that the only thing Gon was given was a message. With nothing to help them get started, the boys set their sights on finding a city.

Back in the real world, Tsezguerra and Battera part ways as Tsezguerra goes to enter the game again himself.

As Gon and Killua walk, they stop and hear a strange noise approaching them. Latarza shoots down from the sky and lands before them. Latarza asks if the boys are new players, noticing that they are so close to the starting location and don't immediately bring out their binders upon seeing him. Killua deflects the question, suspicious of him. Latarza uses Peek to identify the boys when targeting them with the card and then uses Peek to see that Killua's binder is empty. Seeing no use for the boys at the current moment, he targets Killua with Trace to be able to find them later. Killua tries to run away from the spell, not knowing what it will do to him or that he cannot run away from a spell.

After being afflicted with the spell, Killua immediately becomes sinister and intimidating as he confronts Latarza about what he did to him. Before Killua can strike him, Latarza, terrified, uses Return to send him to Masadora. Gon and Killua continue on their way, and although Killua feels no different, the two boys wonder about what kind of spell Latarza used on him. 

Over the hill, the two catch sight of Antokiba. Once there, they see many different job postings around the town offering items as completion prizes. They go over to where a large crowd of people has gathered in front of the town's monthly tournament schedule. They decided to join the Rock Paper Scissors Tournament, which will be held in four days. In the crowd, the boys spot several other players who joined the game with them.

Hungry, the boys make their way to a restaurant, where they participate in a thirty-minute eating challenge in order to win Galgaida. They win the challenge, but cannot get many of their questions answered by the Cat Diner NPC they talk to, as most of their questions are beyond his programmed knowledge. However, after receiving their Galgaida cards, the boys trigger more dialogue with the Cat Diner NPC, who can tell them more about Greed Island cards. 

Despite winning the eating challenge, the boys still have to pay for their sodas. Their real-life currency is not accepted, as they're required to pay with J10,000 cards, and the boys end up having to wash dishes in the back.

As they walk through town, Gon and Killua hear an explosion and rush to see what happened. A crowd of people stands around Jeet's body, whose abdomen has just exploded from a bomb placed inside of him. Gon immediately becomes nervous upon realizing that his father is the type of person to create a game where someone could die so horribly and then die in real life as a result. The boys worry that the spell placed on Killua might result in the same thing that happened to Jeet, but Nickes approaches them to assure them otherwise. He explains that what happened to Jeet was caused by another player's Nen, done as a form of player hunting to reduce the number of players in the game and make it more likely for other people to be able to obtain limited cards. Nickes offers Gon and Killua the chance to join his group, claiming that it guarantees they can win the game.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Trivia

Hiei, a main character from Yoshihiro Togashi's YuYu Hakusho made a cameo appearance in this episode. He can be seen on a poster in Akitoba.

Togashi and Naoko's manga-sonas also appear in the episode, on one of the other job posters hung on a wall in town.
